title: DNA extraction from whole blood using simple salting out procedure

steps: If you use FROZEN blood, start with step-case 'FROZEN blood'. If FRESH blood is used, select step-case 'FRESH blood'.
Decant the blood sample into 50 ml conical tube.
Add 1x RBC lysis buffer up to 50 mL .
Place on shaker at Room temperature for 00:30:00 . Note: Blood solution becomes transparent.
Centrifuge 00:15:00 at 2000 rpm, °C, at Room temperature .

Discard supernatant in container with 20 %, bleach . Note: The pellet contains the nuclei. If you continue with processing "Nuclei lysis , day 1", then continue after this step with Nuclei lysis (step 13) and leave pellet in 50ml conical tube .
Add 700 µL, 1x RBC lysis buffer and close the tube tightly. Pipette up and down to resuspend the pellet (cleans up the nuclei pellet from additional RBCs and allows for storage in small tubes).
Transfer to 1.5 ml pre-labeled clear Eppendorf tube.
Centrifuge 00:05:00 at 2000 rpm, °C, at Room temperature .
Using a 1000 µl pipette tip, discard the supernatant in container with 20 %, bleach .
Pellets can be frozen at this point at -80 °C .
Add 3 mL, Nucleus Lysis Buffer and close tube tightly.
Pipette up and down to resuspend pellet.

Add 300 µL of 10 %, SDS and 70 µL, Proteinase K ( 10 mg/ml stock solution). The total volume is about 3370 plus the volume of the pellet for the nuclei lysis. If 70ul of 10mg/ml proteinase K is added, it results in a dilution of ~1:50, which will be a final proteinase K concentration of 200ug/ml.
Mix by gently swirling tube.

Incubate Overnight in water bath at 55 °C . Temperature is at 55C as proteinase K is resistant to denaturation by heat and will continue to function as long as the temperature/concentration is not too high, wherease most mucleases are denatureated at this temparature.
Add 1 mL of 5 Molarity (M), NaCl to the tubes.
Close caps tightly and shake vigorously . Note: Very important to shake thoroughly at this step to precipitate proteins.
Centrifuge for 00:20:00 at 3000 rpm, °C, at Room temperature .
While centrifuging, label 2x 15ml conical tubes per sample.
Transfer supernatant to a 15 ml conical tube and then repeat centrifugation for 00:20:00 at 3000 rpm, °C, Note: The supernatant contains the DNA.
Transfer supernatant to next 15 ml conical tube.
Add equal volume of ice-cold isopropanol (usually about 4 mL ).
Invert tubes several times and DNA will precipitate. Note: You should be able to see white strings (this is the DNA!). DNA after addition of 5M NaCl and vigorous shaking.
Centrifuge for 00:05:00 at 3000 rpm, °C, .
Discard supernatant.
Add 1 mL 70 %, ethanol to each tube.
Centrifuge for 00:05:00 at 3000 rpm, °C, .
Carefully pipette off ethanol to not dislodge the DNA pellet.
Leave DNA to dry in uncapped tubes overnight.
Add 100 µL to 250 µL, TE and allow DNA to dissolve in 15 ml conical tube stored in fridge ( 4 °C ).
Once completely dissolved , transfer to labeled sterile Eppendorf tube.
Label top with subject ID . Label side with date of extraction and concentration .
Spec all samples using Nanodrop . Dilute samples to stock at 250 ng/μl – 350 ng/μl and to working dilution at 10 ng/μl .
Transfer samples into barcode tubes and log into DNA bank.
In the morning take out tubes of blood from the -80 °C freezer. Record ID numbers and put in fridge ( 4 °C ) to thaw until later in the afternoon.
Once blood has thawed, invert several times or place on rotator for a couple of minutes.
Decant the blood sample into 50 ml conical tube.
Add 1x RBC lysis buffer up to 50 mL .
Place on shaker at Room temperature for 00:30:00 . Note: Blood solution becomes transparent.
Centrifuge 00:15:00 at 2000 rpm, °C, at Room temperature .
Discard supernatant in container with 20 %, bleach . Note: The pellet contains the nuclei. If direct processing with Nuclei lysis , day 1, then continue after this step with Nuclei lysis (step 12) .
Add 700 µL, 1x RBC lysis buffer and close the tube tightly. Pipette up and down to resuspend the pellet (cleans up the nuclei pellet from additional RBCs and allows for storage in small tubes).
Transfer to 1.5 ml pre-labeled clear Eppendorf tube.
Centrifuge 00:05:00 at 2000 rpm, °C, at Room temperature .
Using a 1000 µl pipette tip, discard the supernatant in container with 20 %, bleach .
Pellets can be frozen at this point at -80 °C .
Add 3 mL, Nucleus Lysis Buffer and close tube tightly.
Pipette up and down to resuspend pellet.
Add 300 µL of 10 %, SDS and 70 µL, Proteinase K ( 10 mg/ml ).
Mix by gently swirling tube.
Incubate Overnight in water bath at 55 °C .
Add 1 mL of 5 Molarity (M), NaCl to the tubes.
Close caps tightly and shake vigorously . Note: Very important to shake thoroughly at this step to precipitate proteins.
Centrifuge for 00:20:00 at 3000 rpm, °C, at Room temperature .
While centrifuging, label 2x 15ml conical tubes per sample.
Transfer supernatant to a 15 ml conical tube and then repeat centrifugation for 00:20:00 at 3000 rpm, °C, Note: The supernatant contains the DNA.
Transfer supernatant to next 15 ml conical tube.
Add equal volume of ice-cold isopropanol (usually about 4 mL ).
Invert tubes several times and DNA will precipitate. Note: You should be able to see white strings (this is the DNA!).
Centrifuge for 00:05:00 at 3000 rpm, °C, .
Discard supernatant.
Add 1 mL 70 %, ethanol to each tube.
Centrifuge for 00:05:00 at 3000 rpm, °C, .
Carefully pipette off ethanol to not dislodge the DNA pellet.
Leave DNA to dry in uncapped tubes overnight.
Add 250 µL, TE and allow DNA to dissolve in 15 ml conical tube stored in fridge ( 4 °C ).
Once completely dissolved , transfer to labeled sterile Eppendorf tube.
Label top with subject ID . Label side with date of extraction and concentration .
Spec all samples using Nanodrop . Dilute samples to stock at 250 ng/μl – 350 ng/μl and to working dilution at 10 ng/μl .
Transfer samples into barcode tubes and log into DNA bank.
Rinse blood tube with 1 mL of 1x RBC lysis buffer and add to 50ml conincal tube.
Rinse blood tube with 1 mL 1xRBC lysis buffer and add to 50ml conical tube.
